# "Semi-Infinite Solid — Interactive Demo" {.unnumbered}

A semi-infinite solid ($x \ge 0$) starts at uniform temperature $T_i$.
At $t = 0$, something happens at the surface — the three cases differ only in *what* that something is.
Each plot below shows temperature profiles at five time snapshots.

| Case | Surface BC | Key parameter |
|------|-----------|---------------|
| I    | $T(0,t) = T_s$ | prescribed temperature |
| II   | $-k\,\partial T/\partial x\big|_0 = q_0''$ | prescribed heat flux |
| III  | $-k\,\partial T/\partial x\big|_0 = h(T_\infty - T(0))$ | convection |

---

## Parameters {#sec-params}

### Shared

```{ojs}
//| echo: false

viewof T_i = Inputs.range([-20, 20], {
  value: -5, step: 1,
  label: "Initial temperature Tᵢ (°C)"
})

viewof alpha_exp = Inputs.range([-7.5, -5.7], {
  value: -6.3, step: 0.05,
  label: "log₁₀(α)  [α in m²/s]"
})

viewof k = Inputs.range([0.1, 5.0], {
  value: 1.0, step: 0.1,
  label: "Thermal conductivity k (W/m·K)"
})
```

### Case-specific boundary conditions

```{ojs}
//| echo: false

viewof T_s = Inputs.range([-30, 40], {
  value: 15, step: 1,
  label: "Case I — surface temperature Tₛ (°C)"
})

viewof q0 = Inputs.range([0, 1000], {
  value: 300, step: 10,
  label: "Case II — surface heat flux q₀″ (W/m²)"
})

viewof h_conv = Inputs.range([1, 100], {
  value: 25, step: 1,
  label: "Case III — convection coefficient h (W/m²·K)"
})

viewof T_inf = Inputs.range([-30, 40], {
  value: 20, step: 1,
  label: "Case III — fluid temperature T∞ (°C)"
})
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false

alpha = Math.pow(10, alpha_exp)

html`<p style="font-size:0.9em; margin:0.2em 0 0.8em 0; color:#444">
  α = ${(alpha * 1e6).toFixed(3)} × 10⁻⁶ m²/s &nbsp;|&nbsp;
  k = ${k} W/m·K &nbsp;|&nbsp;
  Tᵢ = ${T_i} °C
</p>`
```

---

## Temperature profiles {#sec-plots}

```{ojs}
//| echo: false

// ── Special functions ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

// erf  (Abramowitz & Stegun 7.1.26, max error ~1.5 × 10⁻⁷)
function erf(x) {
  if (x < 0) return -erf(-x);
  const t = 1.0 / (1.0 + 0.3275911 * x);
  const p = t * (0.254829592 + t * (-0.284496736 + t * (1.421413741
          + t * (-1.453152027 + t * 1.061405429))));
  return 1.0 - p * Math.exp(-x * x);
}

// erfc(x) = 1 − erf(x)
function erfc(x) { return 1 - erf(x); }

// erfcx(u) = exp(u²) · erfc(u)  — numerically stable for all u ≥ 0
// Uses direct computation for u < 5, asymptotic expansion for u ≥ 5.
function erfcx(u) {
  if (u < 0) return 2 * Math.exp(u * u) - erfcx(-u);
  if (u < 5) return Math.exp(u * u) * erfc(u);
  // Asymptotic: erfcx(u) ~ (1/√π)/u · [1 − 1/(2u²) + 3/(4u⁴) − 15/(8u⁶) + ...]
  const t = 1 / (u * u);
  return (1 / Math.sqrt(Math.PI)) / u
       * (1 - t * 0.5 + t * t * 0.75 - t * t * t * 1.875 + t * t * t * t * 6.5625);
}

// ── Depth array ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
x_max = Math.min(30, Math.max(2, 4 * Math.sqrt(alpha * 365 * 86400)));
N_PTS = 300;
x_arr = Array.from({length: N_PTS}, (_, i) => i / (N_PTS - 1) * x_max);

// ── Time snapshots ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
times_days  = [7, 30, 90, 180, 365];
times_s     = times_days.map(d => d * 86400);
time_labels = ["7 days", "30 days", "90 days", "180 days", "1 year"];
time_colors = ["#1f77b4", "#ff7f0e", "#2ca02c", "#d62728", "#9467bd"];

// ── Solution formulas ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

function T_caseI(x, t_s) {
  const eta = x / (2 * Math.sqrt(alpha * t_s));
  return T_s + (T_i - T_s) * erf(eta);
}

function T_caseII(x, t_s) {
  const eta = x / (2 * Math.sqrt(alpha * t_s));
  return T_i
    + (2 * q0 * Math.sqrt(alpha * t_s / Math.PI) / k) * Math.exp(-eta * eta)
    - (q0 * x / k) * erfc(eta);
}

// Numerically stable Case III using erfcx:
// T = Tᵢ + (T∞ − Tᵢ) · [erfc(η) − exp(−η²) · erfcx(η + β)]
// where β = h√(αt)/k
function T_caseIII(x, t_s) {
  const eta  = x / (2 * Math.sqrt(alpha * t_s));
  const beta = h_conv * Math.sqrt(alpha * t_s) / k;
  return T_i + (T_inf - T_i)
    * (erfc(eta) - Math.exp(-eta * eta) * erfcx(eta + beta));
}

// ── Row builder ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
function makeRows(T_fn) {
  return times_s.flatMap((t_s, j) =>
    x_arr.map(x => ({ x, T: T_fn(x, t_s), time: time_labels[j] }))
  );
}

rows_I   = makeRows(T_caseI);
rows_II  = makeRows(T_caseII);
rows_III = makeRows(T_caseIII);
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false

// ── Plot factory ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
function makePlot(rows, title) {
  return Plot.plot({
    width:  215,
    height: 310,
    marginLeft:   48,
    marginBottom: 42,
    marginTop:    28,
    style: { fontFamily: "Georgia, serif", fontSize: "11px" },
    title: title,
    x: { label: "x (m) →", domain: [0, x_max], axis: "bottom" },
    y: { label: "↑ T (°C)", axis: "left" },
    color: {
      domain: time_labels,
      range:  time_colors,
      legend: false
    },
    marks: [
      Plot.gridX({stroke: "#e0e0e0"}),
      Plot.gridY({stroke: "#e0e0e0"}),
      Plot.ruleY([0], {stroke: "#999", strokeDasharray: "4,3", strokeWidth: 1}),
      Plot.line(rows, {x: "x", y: "T", stroke: "time", strokeWidth: 2}),
    ]
  });
}

// ── Legend (shared) ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
legend = Plot.plot({
  width: 660, height: 30,
  marginTop: 0, marginBottom: 0, marginLeft: 0, marginRight: 0,
  color: { domain: time_labels, range: time_colors, legend: true },
  marks: []
});

// ── Three-column layout ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
{
  const p1 = makePlot(rows_I,   "Case I — prescribed Tₛ");
  const p2 = makePlot(rows_II,  "Case II — prescribed q₀″");
  const p3 = makePlot(rows_III, "Case III — convection h");

  const row = document.createElement("div");
  row.style.cssText = "display:flex; gap:12px; align-items:flex-start; flex-wrap:wrap";
  row.append(p1, p2, p3);

  const wrap = document.createElement("div");
  wrap.append(legend, row);
  return wrap;
}
```

---

## What to look for {#sec-observations}

**Case I — erf profile, surface pinned at Tₛ.**
The surface is always exactly $T_s$ (the boundary condition is enforced), and far below the surface the solid is undisturbed at $T_i$.
The transition zone stretches rightward as $\sim\sqrt{\alpha t}$ — same scaling as before.

**Case II — unbounded surface temperature.**
There is no "target" surface temperature here; instead energy is injected at a constant rate $q_0''$.
The surface temperature grows without bound ($\sim \sqrt{t}$), which is physically unrealistic for long times — it is a warning that the semi-infinite model eventually breaks down.
Notice that deeper in the solid the profiles look similar to Case I.

**Case III — convection slows the approach.**
Compared to Case I (same $T_i$ and $T_\infty = T_s$), the surface never quite reaches $T_\infty$ immediately; it approaches it gradually.
A small $h$ means the convective resistance dominates: heat transfer is slow, and the solid barely warms.
A large $h$ drives Case III toward Case I (the surface is cooled/heated so efficiently it acts like a prescribed temperature).

---

## Worked example — Case I thaw depth {#sec-worked}

Frozen soil ($\alpha = 5 \times 10^{-7}\ \text{m}^2/\text{s}$, $T_i = -5\,^\circ\text{C}$) has its surface suddenly raised to $T_s = 15\,^\circ\text{C}$.
How deep does the 0°C thaw front reach after 90 days?

```{ojs}
//| echo: false

ex_alpha = 5e-7;  ex_Ti = -5.0;  ex_Ts = 15.0;  ex_t = 90 * 86400;

function erfinv(y) {
  if (y >=  1) return  Infinity;
  if (y <= -1) return -Infinity;
  if (y <   0) return -erfinv(-y);
  let lo = 0, hi = 6;
  for (let i = 0; i < 60; i++) {
    const mid = (lo + hi) / 2;
    (erf(mid) < y) ? (lo = mid) : (hi = mid);
  }
  return (lo + hi) / 2;
}

ex_target = -ex_Ts / (ex_Ti - ex_Ts);
ex_eta    = erfinv(ex_target);
ex_x_thaw = 2 * Math.sqrt(ex_alpha * ex_t) * ex_eta;

html`
<p><strong>Target:</strong> erf(η) = −Tₛ/(Tᵢ − Tₛ) = ${ex_target.toFixed(4)}</p>
<p><strong>η = erf⁻¹(${ex_target.toFixed(4)}) ≈ ${ex_eta.toFixed(4)}</strong></p>
<p><strong>Thaw depth = 2√(αt) · η ≈
  <span style="color:#c0392b">${ex_x_thaw.toFixed(2)} m</span></strong></p>
<p style="font-size:0.9em; color:#555">
  After 90 days the 0°C isotherm sits at about ${ex_x_thaw.toFixed(1)} m —
  verify this against the "90 days" curve in Case I above
  (with sliders at Tₛ = 15°C, Tᵢ = −5°C).
</p>`
```
